name: API versioning: add v2 without breaking v1
task: Add a /api/v2/users endpoint to /tmp/eval_apiv/app.py. v2 must return the same underlying user data but with renamed fields: 'name' becomes 'firstName' and 'email' becomes 'emailAddress'. v1 must continue to work UNCHANGED — same URL, same field names, same data. Both versions must be served by the same running app. Do not duplicate the user data — derive v2's response from the v1 USERS list at request time. v2 must also support GET /api/v2/users/<index> returning a single user (0-indexed) or a 404 with JSON {'error': 'not found'} for out-of-range indices.

validation.script: import importlib.util, sys; sys.path.insert(0, '/tmp/eval_apiv'); spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location('app', '/tmp/eval_apiv/app.py'); mod = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec); spec.loader.exec_module(mod); client = mod.app.test_client(); r1 = client.get('/api/v1/users'); assert r1.status_code == 200 and r1.get_json() == [{'name': 'Alice', 'email': 'alice@example.com'}, {'name': 'Bob', 'email': 'bob@example.com'}], f'v1 broken: {r1.get_json()}'; r2 = client.get('/api/v2/users'); j2 = r2.get_json(); assert r2.status_code == 200; assert j2 == [{'firstName': 'Alice', 'emailAddress': 'alice@example.com'}, {'firstName': 'Bob', 'emailAddress': 'bob@example.com'}], f'v2 wrong shape: {j2}'; r3 = client.get('/api/v2/users/0'); assert r3.status_code == 200 and r3.get_json() == {'firstName': 'Alice', 'emailAddress': 'alice@example.com'}, f'v2 single: {r3.get_json()}'; r4 = client.get('/api/v2/users/99'); assert r4.status_code == 404 and r4.get_json() == {'error': 'not found'}, f'404: {r4.status_code} {r4.get_json()}'; print('OK')
